I already introduced him. You know he is. You know why he's here. So launch expert of the world and did a really cool job with the certification program, which we haven't talked about much yet, but we will. So I leave you and Jeff's capable hands for a little while. And then I'll be back and we'll do some Q&A. So this is the quick version of Product Launch Formula. But first, before I get into that, basically Product Launch Formula, it's this thing I created. Wow, all of a sudden that sound just blew out at me. It's basically this method of selling that I created. It's to launch a product or relaunch a product. If you launch a business or relaunch a business, this is an online thing. This is what it's basically, it's internet marketing. I mean, it can be used for an offline business. It could be used for an offline service. But it's primarily selling online. Works best for information products, which also includes seminars, membership sites, continuity, anything like that. It also works for e-commerce, for widgets, but the perfect fit is the fit where you just drop it in and get instant money as if you have an information marketing business, especially if you already have a list. Then it's just instant money. So one other thing is, I'm not a professional speaker. I've certainly spoken plenty of times. I'm plenty comfortable up here. But this is something I completely made up this presentation for you folks, because I've never talked about this. I've never revealed this stuff at all. I have a Platinum Group, and I revealed a small portion of it to them on a call once. But otherwise, no one else has ever heard this story. This is a presentation. I have literally put this PowerPoint together over the last few days for you. So some of these slides, they're going to be as big a surprise to me as they are to you as they come out. And some of them, I hate having tons of text on a slide, but some of them, I had to do that because I need to remember what I'm going to tell you. That's really what this PowerPoint is for me to remember what to say. I can go on and on about this stuff, but that's what it's about. OK. I forgot to check what time it was. We're just going to say I got 59 minutes from now. OK, so in the last 58 hours, one of the things with these launches is I don't just teach them. I'm actually pretty good at them. And that's sort of self-deprecating. I'm really, really good at them. And so in the last 58 hours, I actually just did a product launch of my own and closed at midnight Pacific. It closed at 3 o'clock R time. I got up, I set an alarm, got up at like 3.34 and turned off the shopping car. Because that's when we said it was going to end. We gave people extra 34 minutes. And we did $1,534,758 in that 58. Thank you. Now, that's by far not my best 58 hours. But by the nature of this launch, this was sort of a relaunch of already existing products. So like last June, when we launched this product, we did $3.5 million in about the same amount of time. I've done crazy stuff. Like I had a single hour, 60 minutes, where I did $1.1 million in sales. I had a second, one second, where I did over $12,000 in sales. It's pretty fun, because in your shopping cart, you can actually go back and look in the logs and see exactly what you did. And I picked out that one second, where we got like six or seven orders, I forget what it was, all in that very second. It was sort of crazy. And basically what it is is you're priming the pump. You're getting people very, very excited for when you're ready to roll out that product and launch it. So enough about me. This is a typical product launch formula owner. This is Dr. Andrew Jones, who's a veterinarian up in Nelson, British Columbia. He had an ebook, so it was just a PDF he was selling. PDF was selling it sort of on ClickBank, which is sort of lowest common denominator in terms of selling stuff online, in terms of the technology to get you there. And this $27 ebook was doing about, there you go, it was on pet care, it was about taking care of your cats and dogs. He was doing about $500 to $1,000 a month. This was just selling an ebook online. It was more a $500,000 for someone who's working for a living. That's a nice car, right? But he wanted the life-changing thing. He really, he was done with the whole being a vet thing. And so this is what he did. This is his business beforehand, doing that $500 to $1,000 a month. Then he got product launch formula. He put together a launch. He did a $60,000 launch. So he had this small list, he was selling stuff to him, basically month after month. And then he pulled back and he did a launch. What he did was he took his $27 ebook, he turned it into a home study, made it $300, launched it, went to the standard product launch. The sequences sold 200 of them at $300 plus created a continuity program off the back end. Did $60,000. If you do the math, that's like five years of sales in like a week. And so this stuff works and it works in all kinds of different niches. He not only got the immediate cash, he got this continuity income. He doubled the size of his list just through word of mouth. He was part of this as you put out a series of prelaunch content, what we call things that get people excited leading into it. And if you do a good enough job, people will start to pass that around virally and you start to build your list that way. He attracted joint venture partners. He didn't have any of those before. His interaction with the list is a really big thing we do. It's a lot of interaction. Dan was talking earlier today about staying in touch with your client. And one of the things you literally have in a launch, in my recent launch, I had thousands of touches with my clients, thousands of people leaving comments about my videos. And that's basically how I identify what the objections are. This is how anyone doing one of these launches, you identify your objections and what your offer should be in this prelaunch content. And of course, once you figure out what people want, you know, you make that and try to sell it to them. That's the proverbial shooting fish in a barrel. So what markets, this is typically, everyone always wants to know, does that work in my business? The answer is unless you're in an emergency, like a locksmith, you're getting called in the middle of the night, or Dan mentioned the bail bondsman, probably not a good fit for product launch.
